description_long: This is a modern attack helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage painted in a vivid orange color with black markings along the tail boom. The helicopter features a large, tinted cockpit canopy with multiple window panels, providing wide visibility for the crew. Its main rotor consists of four long, straight blades with alternating green and white stripes, while the tail rotor is mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom and also features striped blades. The landing gear consists of two robust skids, each supported by crossbars and attached to the underside of the fuselage. The helicopter's body includes detailed surface features such as panel lines, intakes, and a prominent nose section. The overall design suggests a military or tactical role, suitable for rapid deployment in urban environments.
description: A vivid orange attack helicopter with striped main and tail rotors, a large tinted cockpit, and sturdy landing skids. The body features black markings and detailed surface elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, wide cockpit canopy, and the full span of the striped main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit windows, landing skids, and the tail rotor at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetry of the landing skids.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit, fuselage details, and the tail rotor, with black markings visible on the tail.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades with alternating green and white stripes, appearing to be made of composite material. The blades are slender and extend well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: A vertical tail rotor with four short blades, also striped in green and white, mounted at the end of the tail boom. The rotor is exposed with no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Two robust, straight landing skids made of metal, painted orange to match the fuselage, with crossbars for structural support and simple footpads at the ends.
attribute_cockpit_windows: The cockpit features a large, multi-panel window arrangement with blue-tinted glass. The windows are angular, wrapping around the front and sides, and are framed by the orange fuselage.
